entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2026: Unlocking Next-Level Innovation to AI with SAP Business AI Platform
entry.content:
*Updated on May 15 with SAP Business AI Platform

Hello SAP Community!

Mark your calendars – SAP Sapphire 2026 is here, and it's set to redefine what's possible. This year, the conversation isn’t just about digital transformation. The focus is on mastering your platform journey to drive continuous innovation, achieve measurable business outcomes, and on how organizations should leverage artificial intelligence. It’s going to be a fantastic opportunity to learn, share together, and of course, I’m especially looking forward to celebrating with everyone at the afterparty. I just found out there’s hazelnut chocolate fruit waffles at Magic Kingdom, so I’m extra excited!

From AI-powered development and intelligent automation to seamless integration and strategic platform management, SAP’s Business AI platform (SAP BAIP) stands as the essential foundation. Its unique differentiation lies in deeply embedding AI across your operations while preserving the invaluable data and business application context, thereby turning challenges into tangible opportunities. Sapphire 2026 will be the pivotal event to launch SAP’s strategic vision, highlighting how AI innovations are integral to the enterprises of tomorrow.

 

The Mandate: Connecting Everything, Across the Entire Landscape

In an era when disconnected landscapes continue to hinder progress, SAP’s customers have consistently shown that the platform's true power lies in end-to-end connectivity. Whether it's your CHRO leveraging SAP SuccessFactors employee data with procurement data for newly onboarding employees, or your CFO optimizing financials with SAP S/4HANA together with external third-party benchmark data, or your CPO driving procurement excellence by keeping warehouse data up to date from supply chain partners, the challenge remains: how do you connect these vital functions, including between the AI agents, to move beyond implementation to true business transformation?

This year, you’ll see how SAP Business AI platform serves as the critical foundation for SAP’s business applications, making this connectivity a reality. It's designed to streamline operations, foster innovation, and enable real-time analysis of insights across your entire business landscape.

 

Spotlight on Transformation: Breakthrough Use Cases for a Connected Future

This Sapphire, we're particularly excited to unveil new use cases that exemplify the SAP Business AI platform’s power to address today's most pressing business challenges.

 

1. Integrate HR data and processes with government and third‑party systems

  • The Challenge: Navigating complex and evolving HR regulations, increasing demands for efficiency in HR service delivery, and managing diverse workforces effectively.
  • How the SAP BAIP helps: By integrating HR data in SAP SuccessFactors (or elsewhere) and processes with government and third-party systems through SAP Integration Suite, organizations can ensure compliance with global and local regulations, automate processes, and streamline interactions with third-parties. This connected process frees the HR department from administrative burdens, creating streamlined employee experiences and compliant data.
  • Recommended sessions at Sapphire:
    • Proving the business value of standardizing on SAP Integration Suite | BTP2350: Hear how teams quantify business value after standardizing on SAP Integration Suite: before-and-after metrics, faster time to integrate, fewer incidents, lower operating effort, and readiness for a mixed SAP and third-party software landscape. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • PPG Industries: Building an AI-ready, clean core integration foundation | BTP2638: See how APIs, events, and cloud integration reduce complexity, improve visibility, and establish an AI-ready foundation for future innovation across SAP and third-party systems. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • Building a foundation for AI with an integrated core HR system | HCM1742: Learn how a connected approach to core HR, payroll, workforce management, and HR service delivery can help you streamline your people and business processes while enabling AI readiness. 
      🗓️ADD NOW

2. Drive profitable growth with secure, personalized digital experiences

  • The Challenge: Organizations struggle with complex processes and fragmented access to customer data, facing increasing competition and rising customer expectations. Data silos often lead to slow and reactive decision-making, impacting customer satisfaction and growth.
  • How SAP BAIP helps: SAP Customer Identity and Access Management (SAP CIAM) protects sensitive customer data and meets global compliance requirements. By consolidating and integrating customer data across various systems with SAP’s platform, it enables the creation of secure, personalized digital experiences that overcome data silos. This supports dynamic and tailored customer interactions, enhancing customer acquisition and retention.
  • Recommended sessions at Sapphire:
    • Standardize, secure, and scale with core platform capabilities | BTP1315: Learn how application lifecycle management, security, and interoperability unlock SAP’s Platform to drive agility and growth. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • Invisible security: SAP Customer Identity and Access Management | BTP1316: Discover how SAP Customer Identity and Access Management accelerates your business by harnessing AI-assisted risk analysis to stop threats in real time, protecting customers behind the scenes without disrupting experiences. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • Secure by design: How SAP is redefining agent execution | BTP2641: Learn how SAP is developing the secure execution layer, combining runtime containment with the governance of AI-enabled data fabric solutions from SAP to define production-ready agentic AI.
      🗓️🗓ADD NOW

 

3. Leverage AI Capabilities in ABAP Cloud Development

  • The Challenge: Developers struggle to balance rapid delivery with maintaining code standards, while traditional coding, debugging, and testing consume a significant portion of their time. Complex legacy code also slows productivity.
  • How SAP BAIP helps: Leveraging AI capabilities directly within ABAP Cloud Development, using tools like SAP Joule for Developers, can dramatically increase developer productivity. AI assists with code generation, ensures code quality, and helps your development teams understand complex systems, freeing them to focus on innovation.
  • Recommended sessions at Sapphire:
    • Agentic ABAP with SAP Joule for Developers: Build faster, migrate smarter | JOU1428: Find out how AI agents with ABAP simplify migration, speed up coding, and increase productivity, even in older versions of SAP S/4HANA. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • ABAP Cloud: Entering the new era of ABAP development | BTP2107: The ABAP Cloud development model, agentic development experience in VS Code, the SAP Joule for Developers add-on, and agentic custom code migration join forces for the next era of growth in ABAP development tools. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • Introduction to ABAP Cloud supported by generative AI | BTP1429: Learn what ABAP Cloud is, get insights into the latest technology advancements, and see how generative AI boosts your adoption of ABAP Cloud. 
      🗓️ADD NOW

 

4. Modernize On-Premise Integration to Cloud with PI/PO to SAP Integration Suite Migration

  • The Challenge: Organizations still using SAP Process Integration/Process Orchestration (SAP PI/PO) are facing the impending need to modernize the integration methods. With a complex integration landscape comprising multiple integration solutions, not only does it add complexity, but it also creates greater technical debt.
  • How SAP BAIP helps: Migrating from SAP PI/PO to SAP Integration Suite provides a modern, cloud-native platform that reduces Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) across the integration landscape. This transition can cut the time to set up new interfaces and unlock significant annual cost savings. It's a strategic move that not only streamlines operations but also provides the scalable, agile integration backbone essential for future AI-driven cloud transformation.
  • Recommended sessions at Sapphire:
    • Modernize your strategy: Migrating from PI/PO to SAP Integration Suite | BTP1360: Learn how the suite enables smooth, scalable, and intelligent integrations across cloud and hybrid landscapes. 
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • Under Armour: Enabling real-time commerce with SAP Integration Suite | BTP2138: Under Armour is consolidating MuleSoft, Boomi, and SAP Process Integration and SAP Process Orchestration into SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • Modernize from SAP Process Integration and SAP Process Orchestration | BTP2100: Learn about migration approaches, coexistence options, and governance—plus the benefits customers see after moving, such as faster change cycles and better reliability and monitoring.
      🗓️ADD NOW

 

Mastering Your SAP Business AI Platform Journey: Measure, Optimize, and Guide


To truly maximize your investment in SAP BAIP, it’s crucial to understand where your organization currently stands and how to strategically advance. Sapphire 2026 will highlight the tools and methodologies designed to guide this journey, available on SAP Discovery Center: 

  • SAP Discovery Center: As your central hub for knowledge and hands-on learning, the Discovery Center continues to evolve. Discover, evaluate, and adopt SAP’s Platform and AI to create tailored business solutions and accelerate your platform adoption.

  • Maturity Assessment: Introduced at last year’s SAP TechEd, this comprehensive technical evaluation transforms uncertainty into actionable insights. In just 10-15 minutes, you can get a precise understanding of your organization's platform maturity across five levels, from "Getting Started" to "Center of Excellence." It provides a clear roadmap, justifies further investments, and uncovers hidden efficiencies and innovation opportunities.

We have since expanded the portfolio with new Line of Business AI assessments for domain-specific insights, and an AI Maturity Calculator is in development with IDC that not only assesses your platform AI readiness but also benchmarks you against your industry peers.

  • Recommended sessions at Sapphire:
    • Your platform journey: Maturity assessment and Adoption Best Practices | BTP1549: Explore the new maturity assessment that benchmarks your current state and uncovers optimization opportunities, paired with the proven platform adoption framework for SAP BTP that guides implementation.  
      🗓️ADD NOW
    • BTP101: Comprehensive Overview of SAP’s Platform — Foundations, Development, Integration, and Governance | ASUG105: This intensive 6-hour workshop provides a complete foundational overview of SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and its core capabilities. Participants will explore the BTP ecosystem, from architecture and development frameworks to enterprise integration and data management solutions.
      🗓️ADD NOW

Your Strategic Next Steps at Sapphire 2026: Attend Must-Attend Sessions:

This Sapphire, don't just attend – Seize this opportunity to proactively empower your organization by understanding the strategic future of AI. In addition to use-case specific recommendations, I’ve identified essential sessions to deepen your strategic understanding.

Secure your spot: add these pivotal sessions to your agenda to fully grasp SAP’s AI and platform vision for enterprise innovation.

  • Scaling AI in cloud ERP with SAP's platform | BTP1343: Learn how the AI platform helps your AI in cloud ERP move from experiments to real, repeatable results. 
    🗓️ADD NOW
  • Using autonomous finance to jump-start an intelligent enterprise | BTP1345: Hear how SAP Integration Suite, SAP Build solutions, and the SAP Business Data Cloud solution offer the framework to orchestrate cross-enterprise processes, helping them steer the corporate growth plan. 
    🗓️ADD NOW
  • SAP's AI platform: What's new, what's next, and why it matters | BTP1448: Get a tour of the latest innovations, hands‑on tools for developers and business users, and an early preview of upcoming capabilities shaping the future of AI on the platform. 
    🗓️ADD NOW
  • Design the future of enterprise AI agents with Joule Studio | BTP1449: Discover how Joule Studio enables teams to shape the next era of intelligent enterprises. With an intuitive no‑code experience, create secure, context‑aware AI agents that plan, reason, and act across SAP solutions and beyond.
    🗓️ADD NOW
  • From idea to impact: Orchestrating across the AI agent lifecycle | BTP1450: Get a tour of the latest innovations, hands‑on tools for developers and business users, and an early preview of upcoming capabilities shaping the future of AI on the platform. 
    🗓️ADD NOW
  • The enterprise path to agentic AI | BTP1454: Discover how Joule Studio creates AI solutions from extensions to agentic apps. SAP’s strategy delivers an enterprise-grade intent-to-execution platform that is purpose-built for SAP customers. 
    🗓️ADD NOW
  • Unlock platform value with AI at Cardinal Health and Johnson Controls | BTP1959: Hear how Cardinal Health Inc. and Johnson Controls International plc unlock platform value with AI-enabled application development, integration, automation, and AI services.
    🗓️ADD NOW

See you there!

Sapphire 2026 is your opportunity to discover how SAP Business AI Platform can propel your organization forward to AI adoption, helping you become a truly connected enterprise with governance, ready for whatever the future holds. If you’re at Sapphire, please come see me at booth 152 – BTP1958 and let’s chat!

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Your Feedback Matters - Multi-language captions for SAP Learning
entry.content:
We have exciting news for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition customers - and we need your input to make it even better!

The SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG team are thrilled to announce that we now have the capability to provide multi-language captions for our SAP Learning videos. This is a big step forward in making our many how-to videos on SAP Learning more accessible, inclusive, and impactful for learners around the globe.

These how-to videos are provided supplementary to SAP Learning Journeys and SAP Learning Courses. They bring you deeper how-to expertise with demonstrations on targeted topics.  The aim is to speed up your innovation adoption.

You can see an example of this on Getting Started with SAP Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition – which is now available with captions in English, German, Spanish, and Portuguese (Brazil).

You choose your caption language Captions in the Settings of SAP Learning video player as shown on the video Getting Started with Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - German language is selected and German captions can be seen overlaying the video

But here's the thing — we want YOU to help us prioritize.

Why Multi-Language Captions Matter

Learning complex enterprise software like SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition is challenging enough without the added barrier of a language gap. Whether you're an end user, a consultant, or an IT professional, being able to follow along in your preferred language can make all the difference between confusion and confidence.

Multi-language captions mean:

  • Greater accessibility for non-native English speakers
  • Better knowledge retention when learning in your native language
  • Wider reach and easier reuse for SAP Learning content
  • A more inclusive learning experience for everyone

Which languages would you like to see?

This is just as important as the content itself! Whether it's German, French, Spanish, Simplified Chinese, Japanese, Portuguese, or another language entirely — your feedback will help us shape our roadmap.

Where Do You Want Us to Start?

With dozens of SAP Learning videos already created for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, we want to focus first on what matters most to you. Here are some areas we're considering — but we'd love your thoughts!

Joule Videos

Are the videos covering Joule, SAP's AI copilot, at the top of your list? As AI-driven features become increasingly central to the SAP experience, understanding Joule in your own language could significantly enhance adoption and productivity across your organization.

For example:

  • Getting Started with SAP Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Resolving Missing Business Roles for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

These supplement Joule courses such as New Course: Getting Started with Joule for Business Users and Course: Introducing Joule 

More videos coming soon!

Fiori Videos

Do Fiori videos take priority for you? With SAP Fiori being the primary user interface for SAP S/4HANA, having captions in multiple languages could help countless users navigate/configure/activate/extend/support and make the most of SAP Fiori launchpad, apps, and classic UIs. For example:

Introductions for Business Users, Key Users, and Consultants:

  • Introducing SAP Fiori Launchpad for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Creating SAP Fiori Launchpad Layouts for Custom Business Roles
  • Extending SAP Fiori Object Pages with Adapt UI
  • Extending SAP Fiori List Report Apps with Adapt UI
  • Extending SAP Fiori Overview Pages with Adapt UI

How-to guidance for Administrators, Architects, and Support teams:

  • Patching SAP Fiori (SAPUI5)
  • Activating SAP Fiori Launchpad Using SAP Fiori Foundation Task List
  • Configuring the SAP Fiori Launchpad for SAP S/4HANA
  • Bringing Apps Onto SAP Fiori Launchpad
  • Activating SAP Fiori Content in Custom Business Roles
  • Enabling the 'Share: Microsoft Teams' Functionality for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA
  • Integrating Contact and Presence Info from Microsoft for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA

These supplement Learning Journeys such as Learning Journey: SAP Fiori - System Administration and Learning courses such as Learning the Basics of SAP Fiori and Discovering SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics

Other Videos

Is there another category of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition videos that you feel is more urgently needed? Is there a video you need that you would like to see created? For example, topics in:

  • Finance and Controlling
  • Supply Chain and Procurement
  • Enterprise Asset Management
  • System Administration
  • Configuring Integration with other Cloud solutions
  • Using SAP Activate for your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Projects

Some examples:

  • Configuring Situation Handling for Maintenance Task Lists in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management
  • Implementing Cross-App Navigation Control for Fiori Applications (“Open In” Capability) in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management
  • Enabling Advanced Shipping & Receiving Capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FPS0
  • Setting up the Data Replication Framework for Product Replication from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Sales/Service Cloud Version 2
  • Mastering the Planning and Execution of a Conversion to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (exclusive content)

These supplement Learning Journeys such as Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Sourcing and Procurement, Implementing Asset Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and many more.

What's most valuable to your role and your organization?

This is your opportunity to directly influence what we bring as SAP Learning how-to videos to you and your teams.

Share your Feedback

Please take a moment to share your thoughts by answering these quick questions in the comments below:

  1. Which SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition video topics are your top priority for multi-language captions?
  2. Which specific languages are most important to you or your organization?
  3. Is there anything else you'd like us to consider as we start to add captions.
  4. Do you have proposals for new videos we can consider for our roadmap ?

Every comment counts. Whether you're a customer, an SAP partner, an independent learner or an employee — your voice helps us build a better learning experience for everyone.

Drop your thoughts in the comments below — we can't wait to hear from you!

Becoming a SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Guru

Follow our tag S4HANA RIG - SAP Community for more from the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG

Find more of our blogs, videos, whitepapers and other accelerators in our SAP Community topic pages:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Reducing Structural Friction in SAP FICO to Unlock Real Business Value
entry.content:
FICO is at the center of all SAP financial transactions and most of what a business has to work with to determine their financial status as well as how they are operating effectively and efficiently. So, in a perfect world, it would be very easy to see what is happening within a business from a financial point of view. But, in reality, this does not always happen that way.

Based on what we have seen over time, rarely has SAP FICO not performed as intended. It works, and it works very well and that's why we keep on using it. However, the initial design of the SAP FICO module can be changed by the evolution of the overall system and by numerous decisions made by many different people throughout the years. In this way, the initial structure becomes increasingly complicated, which results in finance teams having greater challenges with the way they are currently able to conduct their daily operational activities.

SAP FICO defines the organization of financial data in a variety of ways. It's not just about keeping transactions but also about having consistency with how those transactions will be organized, controlled and interpreted throughout your organization. When this organization is inconsistent, the ramifications are far reaching and will impact processes, reporting and decision making outside of the system.

In most cases, consistency doesn't happen over night but is gradually built upon over time from various sources. New customizations are made to meet a specific need in the organization, integrations are added as more systems come online and processes are modified to fit the business need. Each of these changes are fine in isolation; however, over time they start to create a level of structural friction that makes things a little more difficult than necessary.

Frictions are easily noticeable in the sense that they start to create sitations nobody really wants to deal with: financial closes take longer to complete, reports need to be manually adjusted prior to being able to rely on them, teams spend more time reconciling the same numbers rather than analyzing them, and when someone needs a concise snapshot of their financials across multiple areas, what should be simple now becomes considerably more complicated.

One project we did with a large energy firm illustrates the same point. We spent time working with their sap systems to organize and combine finance-related data from the many disparate sources of SAP data. Over years of implementation, a large amount of data built up in an uncoordinated manner, and therefore created many inconsistencies across the company.

At first, it appeared that the issue might be due to the data not being available, but that wasn't entirely accurate. Data was in fact present, but in many different formats, extracted by various means, and not at the same level of detail. Many of the departments did not use the same data, nor did they use it consistently.

Related to that, basic analytic activities became very challenging. Every report had to be reformatted and validated, and the time taken to make decisions moved from immediate to much longer due to the effects of the system. Nevertheless, the organization of data within the system created a high level of friction throughout all levels of the company.

In one of our projects, an example of what we found was that operational data coming from the field was fed directly into SAP (the operating system for supply chain management) and automatically caused maintenance and work order processes to be triggered without manual involvement. After establishing the backbone of that integrated process, the end-to-end flow of information could occur without the need for any further validation thereby allowing the process to be completed more quickly and reliably.

It quickly became apparent that this type of friction is very common and that the reason for this kind of friction is generally structural. At its root, we find a human issue since the evolution of systems tends to be a result of different teams making decisions that don’t follow a single, well-thought-out plan.

To ease this friction, you don’t necessarily need to redesign everything from scratchr. In most instances, it would be more beneficial to simplify what already exists. For example, if you align key data structures, eliminate non-essential complexity, and ensure that integrations are based on a consistent logic, they will have a substantially greater positive effect than providing additional tools or layers on top.

SAP FICO is a strong starting point for running finance at present, and the challenge is whether or not it should continue to add on top of it as well as keep the support models for SAP FICO consistent over time.

When the support models are clear, it is easier to trust the data from SAP FICO, and the processes will function smoothly, allowing the teams to make decisions rather than fixing inconsistencies in the system. When they are not, even the simplest tasks will require additional steps, which makes it harder for a user to see value in the system. Our experience shows that growing complexity causes decreased business value, not increased business value.

entry.title: Choosing the Right TMS SAP S/4HANA: Why one Size Rarely Fits all
entry.content:
Recently, I was approached by the supply chain leadership team and the head of IT of one of the top European giants to shortlist five transportation planning solutions. The company was midway through an SAP S/4HANA migration. Their legacy TMS was to be replaced for expiring software and support issues. Every planning vendor was telling them that they can integrate seamlessly with S/4HANA. Although they all integrate, that does not mean it is the right fit.

The chemical company had freight mix like most shippers I worked with in the past, having full truckloads, less than truckloads, and small parcels. They moved FTLs between DCs and major customers. The used LTL carriers for regional replenishment to smaller retail locations. And they shipped thousands of small parcels daily direct to consumers, parts for warranty replacement, or samples through UPS, FedEx, and regional carriers. So fundamentally, three transportation planning problems. They needed one technology to address all of them within an S/4HANA environment.

After working for 18 years with multiple industries implementing SAP Transportation Management and Order to Cash processes across manufacturing, retail, and third-party logistics, I have learned that the biggest mistake companies make in TMS selection is treating it as a single problem. It is rarely one; FTL planning, LTL optimization, and small parcel management, each one by itself, are unique opportunities. Generally, one software decision for all freight modes is often not right.

Every transportation planning stack relies on a central decision-making layer that drives optimization, execution, and responsiveness. The system that holds the optimization logic, the carrier master, the lanes, the rates, and the planning rules. When you run SAP S/4HANA, you have a choice to choose if you want the decision-making inside the SAP ecosystem or if you want it in a best-of-breed external TMS that connects to S/4HANA as its backbone.

This is not a technical question. It is a strategic one. The answer shapes every other decision.

If the decision-making lives in SAP, specifically in SAP TM, which is embedded in S/4HANA, you get native data flows, a single master data model, tight integration between order management, inventory, realistic ATP, and transportation, and no middleware layer to maintain between your planning system and your ERP. The trade-off is generally SAP TM’s optimization capabilities, while robust, are not specialized like some best-of-breed tools that offer transportation-specific reporting, parcel optimization, and overall market freight rate intelligence (highs and lows).

If the decision-making lives in a best-of-breed TMS system a platform like Blue Yonder, MercuryGate, Oracle TMS, or Uber Logistics they typically get more sophisticated optimization engines for specific modes or network configurations as a vendor whose product specifically focuses on transportation. The trade-off is integration complexity, duplicate master data, and the ongoing cost and maintenance support for the connection between your TMS and S/4HANA.

The company was leaning towards best of breed. By the end of the evaluation, they chose SAP TM for their FTL and LTL operations and a dedicated parcel management solution for their small parcel volume integrated into S/4HANA through an API.

 

The three freight mixes are three different decisions.

Full truckload (FTL) planning is fundamentally about lane selection, load optimization, and carrier selection against the freight contracts. The key variables are weight, cube, origin-destination pairs, and service level commitments. SAP TM was designed around this problem and handles it in depth with multi-leg chains, intermodal transfers, freight tendering, and freight order management. For FTL networks of any complexity, SAP TM is a strong native choice within S/4HANA.

Less than truckload (LTL) is a different optimization challenge, as you are not moving a dedicated truck from original to destination; rather, you are sharing trailer space with other shippers, which means your cost and transit time depend on carrier networks. The freight classification by National Motor Freight classification code, dimensions, and accessorial charges that vary by carrier in ways that are difficult to model cleanly. LTL rating is complex because of deficit weight calculation, weight breaks, base rates, discount structure, FAK (Freight All Kinds Agreements) for specific product freight classes, and accessorials like liftgate, inside delivery, and other surcharges interact differently across every carrier.

SAP TM can manage LTL freight orders and carrier selection, but its native LTL rating engine has significant configuration effort to replicate the full complexity of real-world LTL tariff structures. Several clients I have worked with have handled this by integrating a specialized LTL rating API or Idocs with tools like SMC3’s RateWare or a third-party rating engine to feed accurate LTL rates into SAP TM's carrier selection logic, while keeping the freight order and execution management inside SAP TM. This hybrid approach generally preserves the S/4HANA integration benefit while closing the LTL rating gap.

Small Parcel operates under a totally different set of rules. Parcel carriers UPS, FedEx, USPS, DHL, and other regional operators operate on a published rate, dimensional weight, zone pricing, residential and fuel surcharges, and service tiers that vary by carrier. So, the main challenge is not lane planning like it is in freight. It's choosing the right carrier and service for each individual shipment, often at very high volume and in real time.

SAP TM is not really built to compete with specialized parcel platforms in that space. Tools like ShipERP, XPS ship, or EasyPost are made specifically for carrier connectivity, live rate shopping, label printing, and tracking. If a company ships a few hundred parcels or more each day, the better approach is usually a dedicated parcel platform connected to S/4HANA, so S/4HANA can still manage the order, cost, and tracking date without trying to do everything itself.

The real question is not which system should handle every mode. It is how to connect the right tool for each shipping mode back to S/4HANA as the main source of truth for cost, delivery, and customer communication.

For my client, that changed the whole evaluation. Instead of trying to force one TMS to cover everything, we designed a layered setup. SAP TM for FTL and managed LTL, a parcel platform for small parcels, and a clean integration model that kept S/4HANA as the central record for delivery and freight cost.

Selecting a transportation management solution for an S/4HANA environment is not just about vendor demos or license price. There are various factors to know, starting from how well the system can fit the business processes, how it integrates with S/4HANA, and whether the users can actually adopt and trust it.

Integration: A TMS system that can only exchange the data with S/4HANA in batches is very different from one that works in real time. In transportation, especially for outbound orders and freight settlement, delayed or unclear integration often can lead to manual work, data mismatches, and rework. Changes in demand documents are another aspect of planning, with the source of truth being the ERP system and freight cost record that stay aligned across finance and logistics.

 

Planning: FTL fits well with SAP TM, while LTL depends heavily on rating accuracy and carrier tariff logic. A small parcel is different from both as it requires real-time rate shopping, service level, label generation, and carrier API connectivity at very high speed. The key is to match the right tool to the mode instead of choosing one system to solve for all equally.

 

Carrier Connectivity: Some best-of-breed platforms have strong carrier networks and can connect more easily to booking, tracking, and invoicing processes. A real advantage for companies who have frequent carrier onboarding needs. SAP’s Business Network for Logistics capabilities have improved, but some specialist platforms offer broader carrier reach in some markets.

User Adoption: Transportation planners work under huge time constraints at times, handling exceptions and needing quick turnaround; they need a system that is intuitive and reliable. If the user interface is too complex, planners will build workarounds in spreadsheets and phone calls, which will defeat the purpose of the whole solution. End-user testing being part of the evaluation is not always a decision for later.

The best transportation solution is one that matches the freight complexity, integrates cleanly with S/4HANA, manages master data well, keeps total cost under control, and is actually usable by the planners who run the business every day.

After the evaluation, the client chose a layered solution instead of trying to use one system for everything. This decision helped the freight network that was too mixed and complex in a single platform approach.

For FTL and international multi-modal freight, they selected SAP TM embedded in S/4HANA. This made sense because their network included ocean and air freight across 14 countries, multi-leg chains, and customs milestones. SAP TM fit that complexity well, and since the master data governance in S/4HANA was already strong, they did not want to add the additional burden of managing a separate transportation system.

For LTL, they kept SAP TM as the main execution platform but connected it to a third-party LTL rating engine through an API. This gave them more accurate carrier-specific pricing and better handling of accessorials while still keeping the S/4HANA delivery document as the main record. The integration worked well because the rating engine acted like a service rather than trying to own the planning process itself.

For small parcels, they chose a dedicated parcel management solution and connected to S/HANA using outbound delivery data. This platform handled real-time rate shopping across multiple carriers and label generation and carrier-specific compliance needs as well. Shipment cost and tracking information flowed back into S/HANA so that finance and customer service still have visibility and also transmit to end customers in the system.

One of the vendors claimed it could cover all three modes, but the proof of concept showed the LTL rating logic could not handle it without manual interference and was easy to knock down.

This is not a recommendation for every company. An organization with simple domestic FTL and little or no parcel volume might do just fine with one good TMS. But the right choice always depends on the company’s freight mix, how complex the network is, how much IT support is available, and how solid the master data is.

The main idea is simple: Use SAP TM where it fits best, use specialized tools where they bring real value, and keep S/4HANA as the core system that ties everything together.

entry.title: ABAP AI: Creating Custom Generative AI Scenarios in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Artificial Intelligence in ABAP – Ready for Today

Artificial Intelligence capabilities are no longer limited to external services or side-by-side extensions. With recent SAP S/4HANA releases, AI scenarios can be consumed and modeled directly within ABAP, allowing existing applications and processes to be enhanced with Generative AI in an enterprise-ready way.

At the center of this enablement is the ABAP AI SDK powered by Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM). It provides the runtime APIs and lifecycle concepts required to define, manage, and consume AI scenarios from ABAP code in a consistent manner. This makes it possible for customers and partners to build Custom AI scenarios inside their own SAP S/4HANA systems, closely aligned with business logic and system architecture.

Why ABAP AI SDK and ISLM Matter

The ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM, enables ABAP developers to interact with Generative AI models without embedding provider-specific logic into applications. ISLM acts as the central framework and lifecycle component for AI scenarios, while the ABAP AI SDK exposes standardized APIs to consume these scenarios at runtime.

Beyond pure large language model (LLM) interactions, SAP AI Core provides an orchestration service. Orchestration in SAP AI Core is a managed service that enables unified access, control, and execution of generative AI models through standardized APIs, prompt templating, and configurable AI workflow components.

Using orchestration, LLM interactions can be enriched with additional capabilities such as data masking, content filtering, document grounding, and translation. The ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM in ABAP can leverage both direct LLM interactions and orchestration features, allowing ABAP applications to consume advanced AI workflows while keeping governance and lifecycle management aligned with SAP standards. 

These capabilities are available as of SAP S/4 HANA 2023.

Learn by Doing: An SAP Developer Mission

To provide a structured, hands-on entry point, we offer an SAP Developer Mission that guides you through the setup and usage of Custom AI scenarios in ABAP.

The mission consists of a tutorial group, guiding you how to set up your SAP S/4 HANA system for custom AI scenarios and dedicated tutorials demonstrating how Custom GenAI scenarios are built and consumed from ABAP using the ABAP AI SDK, including orchestration-based interactions with generative models.

Who Is This Relevant For?

The mission is intended for:

  • ABAP developers who want to understand how Generative AI can be consumed from ABAP
  • Architects evaluating AI integration options for SAP S/4HANA
  • Development teams exploring Custom AI scenarios within their existing SAP landscapes

No prior AI expertise is required, but familiarity with ABAP development is assumed.

Getting Started

Ready? Let's explore the SAP Developer Mission 

👉Get Started with GenAI Development in SAP S/4HANA 

which provides a guided and practical introduction on  Generative AI scenario creation and consumption in ABAP.

Want to Learn More?

  • SAP Help Portal: ABAP AI & ISLM Documentation
  • SAP Community Page: Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management for SAP S/4HANA 
  • SAP Community Blog: Scaling Generative AI in SAP S/4HANA Enterprise-Ready Innovations with ISLM 
  • SAP Community Blog: Developing AI‑enabled applications with the ABAP AI SDK (Public Cloud & Steampunk)

entry.title: SAP Joule in Action: Redefining Business Intelligence
entry.content:
Introduction

The evolution of enterprise systems is entering a new phase one where AI is intergarted and actively  collaborates with users. With the introduction of SAP Joule, SAP has positioned itself at the forefront of this transformation by delivering a generative AI copilot across business applications.

SAP Joule AI represents a paradigm shift in how users interact with SAP systems such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors.

working on AI transformations, I have observed a clear shift: business users are no longer satisfied with transactional systems they expect intelligent assistants embedded directly into workflows. Joule aligns exactly with this expectation, bridging the gap between business intent and system execution.

What is SAP Joule?

SAP Joule is a generative AI copilot and more embedded across SAP’s cloud portfolio. It enables users to:

  • Interact with SAP systems using natural language
  • Automate complex workflows
  • Generate insights in real time
  • Enhance decision-making with contextual intelligence

 

Built on the SAP Business Technology Platform, Joule leverages AI models integrated with enterprise data while ensuring governance, security, and compliance.

From my implementation experience, one of the biggest challenge in SAP systems has been user navigation complexity. Joule’s conversational interface eliminates the need for deep transaction code knowledge, which is particularly valuable in large-scale deployments involving non-technical users.

Why SAP Joule Matters

Traditional ERP systems are transactional. SAP Joule makes them conversational and intelligent.

 

Key Value Drivers:

  • Productivity Boost: Reduce manual effort through AI-assisted automation
  • User Experience Transformation: Shift from UI-driven to intent-driven interactions
  • Faster Decision-Making: Real-time contextual insights
  • Process Optimization: Intelligent recommendations embedded in workflows

During SAP S/4HANA transformation programs, especially in success factor / supply chain environments, decision-making often depends on multiple reports and manual analysis. Joule has the potential to compress decision cycles from hours to minutes by generating real-time insights directly within the process

SAP Joule Architecture Overview

 

 

A typical Joule-enabled landscape includes:

Core ERP Layer

  • SAP S/4HANA (Digital Core)

AI & Extension Layer

  • SAP BTP (AI services, integration, extensions)

Data Layer

  • SAP Datasphere / SAP HANA Cloud

AI Copilot Layer

  • SAP Joule embedded in Fiori applications

Integration Layer

  • APIs, OData services, Event Mesh

 In S/4HANA  implementations , maintaining a clean core is critical. Joule fits well into this paradigm because it leverages BTP for AI processing, avoiding heavy customizations in the core ERP system.

Implementation Approach for SAP Joule

Phase 1: Readiness Assessment

  • Evaluate system landscape (ECC vs S/4HANA)
  • Identify high-value AI use cases
  • Assess data quality and governance maturity

 

Data readiness is the single biggest success factor. Without harmonized master data and clean transactional data, AI outputs , no matter how advanced , lose business relevance.

Phase 2: Platform Enablement

  • Activate SAP BTP services
  • Configure AI capabilities
  • Ensure connectivity with S/4HANA

Architectural side treat BTP not just as a platform, but as an AI innovation layer. Joule implementation makes it central to the architecture.

Phase 3: Use Case Identification

Common SAP Joule use cases:

Supply Chain

  • Demand forecasting insights
  • Exception handling automation

Procurement

  • Smart supplier recommendations
  • Contract summarization

HR

  • Job description generation
  • Leave insights

 

I see immediate value in navigation  where Joule can proactively suggest actions instead of users searching to find the panel.

Phase 4: Integration & Extension

  • Extend Joule capabilities using SAP BTP
  • Integrate with external AI models if required
  • Enable APIs for custom workflows

In complex enterprise landscapes (e.g., SAP + external logistics platforms), Joule can act as a unified intelligence layer, reducing dependency on multiple dashboards and tools.

Phase 5: Security & Governance

  • Role-based access control
  • Data privacy compliance
  • AI explainability and auditability

From an enterprise architecture standpoint, I see AI governance is not optional. Organizations must define:

  • What data Joule can access
  • How decisions are audited
  • How AI outputs are validated

This has become especially critical in regulated industries.

Phase 6: Change Management

  • User training for conversational AI
  • Adoption strategy
  • Continuous feedback loop

One underestimated challenge is user trust in AI. Adoption improves significantly when users understand:

  • How Joule generates responses
  • When to trust vs validate outputs

Best Practices for SAP Joule Implementation

  • Start with Business Value, Not Technology
  • Prioritize Clean Core in S/4HANA
  • Leverage Standard SAP AI Capabilities First
  • Ensure High Data Quality
  • Adopt Agile AI Deployment Approach

We Started with assistive AI (recommendations) before moving to autonomous AI (decision-making). This phased approach reduces risk and improves adoption.

Challenges to Consider

  • Data readiness and harmonization
  • User adoption resistance
  • AI governance and compliance
  • Integration complexity in hybrid landscapes

In real projects, the biggest blocker is not technology it’s organizational readiness. AI transformation requires alignment across IT, business, and leadership.

Future Outlook

SAP Joule will evolve into a central AI orchestrator, enabling:

  • Autonomous business processes
  • AI-driven decision intelligence
  • Multi-agent enterprise ecosystems

Given my work in Agentic AI, I see Joule as a foundation for multi-agent enterprise systems, where different AI agents collaborate across supply chain, finance, and operations.

Conclusion

SAP Joule represents a shift from transactional ERP systems to intelligent, AI-driven enterprises.

For SAP architects and leaders, the focus should be on aligning AI capabilities with business outcomes, ensuring governance, and building scalable AI-driven ecosystems.

From an SAP architect’s strategic perspective, Joule is not just another feature it is a strategic capability that will redefine how users interact with enterprise systems. Organizations that successfully adopt it will gain a significant competitive advantage in automation, decision-making, and operational efficiency.

entry.title: Is SAP CPE the Missing Answer to Geopolitical Raw Material Price Volatility?
entry.content:
Raw material prices do not wait for purchase orders to close. That gap between when a PO is raised and when goods actually arrive has always created friction between Procurement and Finance but in today's environment, with commodity markets reacting to sanctions, shipping disruptions, and currency swings, the gap has become expensive.

This article documents personal technical research into SAP Commodity Pricing Engine (CPE) and what it actually offers organizations running S/4HANA in commodity-intensive environments. The exposure here is research and architecture-based rather than live implementation, but the process logic is worth walking through in detail.

The Operational Problem

The core issue is timing.

A Purchase Order gets created at today's market rate. Goods Receipt happens three weeks later. The supplier invoice arrives two weeks after that. By then, the LME copper price or Platts crude benchmark has moved, and none of the original figures hold.

What typically fills that gap in practice:

  • Manual invoice adjustments processed outside the system
  • Inventory valuation entries that do not reflect actual procurement cost
  • Back-and-forth reconciliation between AP and Procurement
  • Period-end corrections that obscure the true financial effect of price movement

The organization ends up managing market volatility through human effort rather than system logic. CPE exists to change that.

How CPE Structures the Pricing Lifecycle

 

CPE replaces static price conditions with formula-based pricing that moves through defined stages across the procurement cycle.

Stage 1  Provisional Pricing at PO Creation

When the PO is created, the system applies a provisional value while leaving the pricing condition open for future recalculation. From a finance perspective, this is the right approach. It allows the company to recognize a procurement commitment without locking an unreliable price into inventory valuation at the earliest and least certain point in the process.

Stage 2  Revaluation During Goods Receipt

During MIGO posting, the system can recalculate pricing based on quotation dates and market reference logic tied to the actual delivery timeline. In environments where commodity prices shift daily, this means inventory is valued at a price that reflects when the goods actually arrived not when the order was raised.

The standard integration supporting this revaluation is more mature than commonly assumed, and does not require the level of custom development that many consultants would initially expect.

Stage 3  Differential Invoicing in MIRO

The staged structure provisional, differential, final settlement means that pricing differences are processed through defined system steps rather than landing as unplanned corrections in Accounts Payable.

In high-volume procurement environments, this matters operationally. AP teams spend real time chasing and correcting invoice variances. A structured differential invoicing process reduces that workload considerably.

The Material Ledger Connection

The most technically significant part of this research was understanding how CPE interacts with Material Ledger and Actual Costing.

Even when procurement prices are changing throughout the period, the resulting variances still flow into Material Ledger settlement during period-end closing  CKMLCP runs, cost component splits, multilevel settlement. The commodity volatility does not disappear into manual adjustments. It shows up as actual cost data that management can analyze.

That integration means CPE is not only a procurement pricing tool. From a FICO perspective, it functions as a financial control mechanism that keeps valuation accuracy intact across a procurement cycle that spans multiple weeks and multiple price points.

What Realistic Implementation Requires

CPE is not a feature that gets switched on during a standard S/4HANA activation. The complexity is real.

Successful implementation depends on several areas working together:

  • Pricing formula design that accurately maps to actual business procurement scenarios
  • Clear ownership of quotation management and market data governance in operations
  • Reliable integration with external quotation providers such as LME or Platts
  • Activation of the relevant business functions, including LOG_CPE_SD_MM
  • Sustained collaboration between Procurement, Finance, and IT throughout the project

One area that remains open for further investigation is how organizations govern quotation data in productive environments  specifically who owns the market reference data, how it is validated, and how discrepancies are handled when external feeds are delayed or corrected.

That operational dimension may explain why CPE remains underutilized relative to what the standard functionality can actually deliver.

Closing Perspective

The strongest argument for CPE is not the pricing mechanics themselves. It is that the entire approach is built around connecting procurement execution directly to financial valuation through standard processes rather than custom workarounds built on top of a system that was not designed for them.

For organizations operating in commodity-driven markets, that connection has real financial value. The question is whether implementation programs are structured to support it properly.

Perspectives from consultants with live CPE experience are genuinely welcome:

  • Where did the configuration complexity actually surface in practice?
  • How did the Material Ledger integration hold up during real period-end closing?
  • Is CPE still being passed over inside S/4HANA programs that would clearly benefit from it?

References

  1. SAP Help Portal – Commodity Pricing Engine (CPE) https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/8308e6d301d54584a33cd04a9861bc52/311e58a9317c4341a1f9d43ba9fc9349.html

  2. SAP Help Portal – Logistics, Commodity Pricing Engine in SD and MM https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/77c07c8d30664260a0b3ff864e6b5e78/7f72f9a33cbb4d01bcccced9ab40a7bf.html

  3. SAP Help Portal – Material Ledger and Actual Costing https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ERP/56f7319a9048445eb86221af73cab72b/bcd6cc5340487214e10000000a174cb4.html

  4. SAP – Commodity Management for Finance and Procurement https://www.sap.com/products/scm/commodity-management.html

The technical observations, process analysis, and conclusions in this article are entirely the author's own, developed through direct review of SAP documentation and architectural research. AI writing tools were used selectively to support image creation, sentence clarity and article structure not to generate the technical content itself.

Mohammed Abood

Senior SAP FICO Consultant

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) Modernization Partner Demo Systems Reprovisioning
entry.content:
SAP is modernising all ECP Partner Demo Systems — moving from the older ERP HCM (ECC) stack to the powerful, future-ready S/4HANA HCM platform. This means faster performance, greater scalability, and a demo experience that truly reflects the innovation your customers expect.

What’s In It For You?

  • A brand-new, high-performing demo environment on S/4HANA HCM
  • Impress prospects with a modern platform that showcases ECP at its very best
  • Future-proof your demos with long-term SAP platform support
  • No disruption — your existing system stays live for a full 4 weeks while you transition

This is a reprovisioning, not a conversion — you’ll receive a fresh system with a new SID and URL, ready to go.

Your Transition Timeline at a Glance

PhaseWhat Happens
Request SubmittedSubmit via the SAP Partner Cloud Ops Portal
New System Goes LiveFresh S/4HANA HCM environment delivered
4-Week Overlap BeginsMigrate, configure, and validate — both systems run in parallel
Legacy System RetiredAutomatic decommissioning — your modern system takes centre stage

Here’s Your Fast-Track Checklist

Before you submit, make sure you’re set up for success:

  • Transports Ready — Workbench and Customizing transports prepared and S/4HANA compatible
  • Team Assigned — Functional and technical resources available to import, configure, and test
  • Plan Committed — All migration and validation activities completed within 1 month of go-live

How to Request Your New Demo System

  1. Log in to the SAP Partner Cloud Ops Portal using your partner credentials
  2. From the top navigation, select Support
  3. Locate and select ECP Partner Demo Reprovisioning
  4. Complete the request template in a Word document and attach it to the ticket
  5. Ensure your submission aligns with your data center-specific timelines
  6. The Provisioning ticket to be created 10 days ahead of the scheduled date of your reprovisioning

After Your New System Is Live

Once your new S/4HANA HCM demo system is provisioned, raise a support ticket under the component LOD-EC-ECP-OPS and subject as “S4 Partner Demo Reprovisioning - Transport request - <SID>" to request :

  • Import of source system transports (Workbench, Customizing, and Add-ons where applicable)
  • Support for system migration and movement activities
  • Provide a full list of transports to be imported
  • Identify any network or integration considerations (e.g., SNC, interfaces, EC–ECP integrations)

Key Reminders

The legacy ERP HCM demo system will not be extended beyond the 4-week overlap period. Decommissioning is automatic — ensure all activities are completed on time.

Questions?

A webinar session is scheduled for June 9, 2026, from 14:00 to 15:00 UTC. You can register for it here.
For any questions or clarifications, please reach out to the SAP ECP Modernization team at: SAP_SuccessFactors_ECP_Modernization@sap.com

We’re committed to making this transition as smooth as possible and look forward to supporting you through this modernization journey.

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Control Tower: Q1-Q2 2026 Updates & Roadmap Highlights
entry.content:
Introduction

ESG reporting software is moving from an emerging category to a mission-critical business capability, driven by regulation, finance-led operating models, and a clear shift in buyer expectations. Even as regulatory scope evolves, demand remains strong: organizations still need to maintain or expand sustainability reporting, strengthen data integrity, and prepare for recurring disclosure requirements.

At the same time, manual and fragmented reporting models are no longer sustainable. Sustainability teams continue to face growing time burdens, computational complexity, and error risk when ESG data is collected across spreadsheets and disconnected processes. This is increasingly a finance-led concern as well: CFOs and finance leaders are becoming more influential in ESG funding decisions and are placing greater emphasis on the integrity of non-financial data.

AI is raising expectations even further. Business leaders increasingly expect reporting processes to become faster, more automated, and more intelligent. Still, sustainability AI can only create trusted business value when it is grounded in governed, traceable, enterprise-grade ESG data. Without this foundation, AI-assisted reporting can accelerate drafting, but it cannot reliably explain, evidence, or defend the data behind the disclosure.

This is where SAP Business AI becomes central to SAP Sustainability Control Tower. SAP’s approach is not AI added on top of disconnected sustainability data. It is AI embedded into business processes, supported by enterprise context, governed workflows, and traceable data from SAP and non-SAP systems. SAP Sustainability Control Tower is designed to support this journey by centralizing sustainability data and insights, helping organizations ensure compliant reporting, embed sustainability into core processes, and drive measurable progress across the enterprise.

These latest updates build on that foundation. They strengthen the data, integration, workflow, analytics, and reporting capabilities that make AI-assisted sustainability management credible in practice.

Here’s what’s new in SAP Sustainability Control Tower as of Q2 2026:

Expanded coverage of pre-delivered regulatory scope

To help organizations accelerate their sustainability reporting readiness, SAP Sustainability Control Tower continues to expand its SAP-provided ESG reporting content. These enhancements support customers by providing additional value for ESRS metric coverage across existing SAP-owned and custom metrics.

Recent master data enhancements include new and enhanced dimensions, measures, and data points. For dimensions, new and enhanced content includes gender attributes, human rights incident values, grievance mechanism recipient types, and severity of human rights incidents. For measures, new content includes human rights incidents, monetary sanctions due to human rights incidents, and enhanced naming for the count of governance body members.

SAP-provided metric coverage for ESRS data points has also been significantly expanded. As of April 2026, SAP Sustainability Control Tower includes 200+ quantitative metrics and 650+ qualitative metrics, supporting organizations with a broader set of content for ESRS data-point requirements. This includes quantitative coverage across areas such as climate change, pollution, water and marine resources, biodiversity and ecosystems, resource use and circular economy, own workforce, and business conduct. Qualitative coverage spans ESRS 2 general disclosures and topical standards across environmental, social, and governance topics. Documentation is available in Help for SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

That growing catalog gives customers a stronger starting point, while still leaving room to extend the model for company-specific or industry-specific reporting needs.

Data collection process enhancements

Governed ESG reporting depends on clear ownership, timing, and workflow. SAP Sustainability Control Tower supports form-based data collection for scoped measures and metrics, helping organizations define how data should be collected, when it should be collected, and who should provide and approve the submitted information.

With the data collection process, users can define data collection forms and set up data collection instances to trigger workflow-based data collection for selected items. They can assign processors for a given period and define approvers to review submitted data for approval or rejection.

Recent enhancements extend support to qualitative metrics in form-based data collection. Users can group qualitative metrics within a single form, and when new fiscal variants are activated, existing forms linked to inactive fiscal variants are automatically deactivated. In addition, table headers remain visible while scrolling, making larger data collection forms easier to use.

The qualitative metric data entry experience has also been redesigned. Users can select from all available metrics and add data at the high-level corporate summary level or at a more granular reporting structure level. Relevant details are available in the reference section on the data entry screen, helping users understand the context for the information they are entering.

The reporting line is now also available as an organizational boundary in data collection. Users can create a reporting scope using the reporting line organization type when setting up organizational boundaries, then run “check for updates” in the Configure Data Collection or Configure Data Entry app to add the new entries to the configurable items list.

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management integration

SAP Sustainability Control Tower also enhances integration with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management for greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions data. Customers can import GHG emissions data from SAP Sustainability Footprint Management into SAP Sustainability Control Tower while preserving native source granularity.

The integration helps eliminate the need for external data transformation and aggregation. Period type can be configured per integration scenario, with supported period types including monthly, quarterly, and yearly based on the active fiscal year variant. Data is ingested into SAP Sustainability Control Tower at the selected granularity, without forced transformation, and replicated to target systems using the configured period type.

Recent enhancements include a new UI control for period granularity selection and configurable period selection, including single-period and period-range options. Fiscal year variant support also helps enable more accurate period mapping.

The same principle of flexibility applies to the broader ESG data model, where customers often need to adapt standard content to their own structures and reporting practices.

ESG Data Foundation extensibility

As sustainability reporting requirements evolve, organizations need flexibility to adapt data models, extend content, and transport configuration across environments. SAP Sustainability Control Tower provides an open, extensible ESG Data Foundation that supports both standard SAP content and customer-specific extensions.

Customers can add custom measures across Data Provider Interfaces and add up to 20 custom dimensions for SAP-provided or custom measures. The content lifecycle for extension artifacts supports custom measures, dimensions, and metrics, with export and import via JSON file format.

Recent enhancements improve the content lifecycle for extension artifacts. Export and import are available in the Manage Metrics, Manage Measures, and Manage Custom Dimensions apps. Export selection can be based on naming conventions and includes dependent custom measures and custom dimensions to help ensure consistency.

Import supports consistency in the target tenant during object creation, and can now also update existing extensibility artifacts.

This makes extension management more practical across systems and tenants, especially as ESG reporting models continue to evolve.

SAP Datasphere content coverage

SAP Sustainability Control Tower also keeps enhancing its integration with SAP Datasphere. Users can leverage SAP Datasphere integration to load actual data into the Manage Quantitative ESG Data app and to upload master data for reporting structures, such as business locations, legal entities, and reporting lines, into the Manage Master Data app.

The SAP Datasphere content package supports multiple Data Provider Interfaces, including accounting, corruption incidents, emissions, generic measures, general measures, injury, land site, people, resource use, water withdrawal, waste, energy, and removal reversal carbon credit. Recent enhancements include updates for emissions and people DPIs, as well as new content for removal reversal and carbon credit scenarios.

The next step is to make those connected data flows easier to apply in regulation-specific scenarios, such as the EU Taxonomy.

EU Taxonomy data integration enhancements

For EU Taxonomy reporting, SAP Sustainability Control Tower now offers enhanced capabilities to manage master data and measures data in a more integrated way.

In the Manage Master Data app, users can configure and manage master data and EU Taxonomy filters in one place. This includes managing company code values used by EU Taxonomy in the legal entity dimension, defining filters applied to SAP S/4HANA master data and activity data, importing master data and hierarchies from files, and importing master data and hierarchies from SAP S/4HANA with the SAP Data Framework.

 

In the Manage Quantitative ESG Data app, users can import EU Taxonomy measures data, including activity data and denominator data. The integration supports imports from files and SAP S/4HANA, with validation rules to check consistency against master data and filters defined in the Manage Master Data app.

This simplifies how EU Taxonomy data is prepared, imported, validated, and exposed to downstream processes, while reducing the need for separate replication across the Manage Master Data app, the Manage Quantitative ESG Data app, and CPE-UM processes.

Analyze ESG Data app enhancements

SAP Sustainability Control Tower continues to evolve the Analyze ESG Data app from a reporting experience into a steering experience. The app helps users explore ESG performance with more flexible analysis, personalization, and target comparison capabilities.

Recent updates introduce target-versus-actual visualization in charts for selected metrics, periods, and reporting structures. This enables immediate performance comparison without additional configuration.

 

Users can also benefit from a flexible chart-specific configuration model that allows independent control over metric selection, filters, drilldowns, chart types, reusable public or private configurations, default views, and embedded descriptions for context.

The multi-chart analytics experience has also been enhanced. Users can display up to six charts in parallel, combine chart and table views, and adjust drilldowns directly in charts. This supports faster exploration and side-by-side analysis across different sustainability performance perspectives.

Generate ESG Reports with AI enhancements

The Generate ESG Reports with AI app matures as a way to reduce manual effort while giving users more control over ESG report creation. Users can generate ESG reports using prebuilt templates and AI-assisted drafting to support timely, accurate, and consistent reporting.

When generating a report, users can preview or download the result as a PDF. For added customization, users can manually edit sections, and reports are now available in DOCX format.

SAP’s built-in AI assistance supports dynamic revisions. Users can select a passage and use the Revise option to receive suggestions to shorten or lengthen the text, helping refine the final ESG narrative with greater ease and speed.

In addition, each report can be generated in one of 20 languages using AI-based translation. 

Planned features and roadmap highlights

Forward-looking statement: The following roadmap items are planned and may change.

Looking ahead to the remainder of 2026, SAP Sustainability Control Tower plans to continue strengthening regulatory coverage and embedded AI capabilities for audit-ready ESG reporting. 

Planned topics include further coverage of ESRS metrics, reporting scope management with inbound interfaces for materiality assessment results, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management integration for energy consumption, unit of measure management, evidence management for data collection, availability of sustainability metrics data product in SAP Business Data Cloud, integration with SAP Success Factors through SAP Business Data Cloud, validation rules, SOC 2 certification and user experience improvements for the overall reporting flow.

SAP Sustainability Control Tower also plans to expand support for IFRS sustainability disclosure requirements. Planned IFRS content provisioning includes comprehensive IFRS coverage, compatible metric definitions that enhance the ESRS offering, and IFRS-specific metric definitions.

Moreover, SAP Sustainability Control Tower is designed to support interoperability across sustainability reporting standards.  Within metric general information, customers can see interoperability between disclosure requirements across standards such as ESRS and IFRS, helping organizations address multiple reporting frameworks through a single reporting effort.

 

SAP also continues to explore AI-enabled support for sustainability regulatory readiness. The Sustainability Regulatory Readiness Agent is one of the planned agents, designed to help accelerate governed, audit-ready sustainability reporting by automating the assembly of reporting scope based on materiality assessments, creating data points for new regulations, mapping existing metrics, and proposing custom metrics where coverage gaps exist. 

Together, these planned capabilities point to a future where sustainability teams can move from interpreting regulatory change manually to operationalizing it with intelligent, governed support, helping organizations respond faster, reduce reporting risk, and build confidence in every disclosure.

Stay tuned for more and check SCT Roadmap Explorer.

Additional information

For in-depth details on SAP Sustainability Control Tower, we recommend browsing the SAP Help Portal. For an overview of all new features and functions, check the What’s New section, which is updated with every new software release.

entry.title: Connecting SAP S/4HANA with SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh via Proxy Configuration
entry.content:
This blog post intends to complement the existing information on the following blog posts:

SAP S/4HANA integration with SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh

SAP S/4HANA and SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh (AEM) direct integration

 

Whenever a proxy server is configured for SAP S/4HANA outbound traffic, certain configuration changes must be made, while other changes must be requested through a Service Request.

If your customer landscape uses a proxy server for outbound traffic that must be maintained in the SM59 destination, and you receive the following error when activating the AEM channel, this means that your RFC destination of type G does not support TCPS connectivity through a proxy server. In this case, the procedure for routing outbound traffic for your Event channels will be different. One possible solution is to implement a load balancer to route events from SAP S/4HANA, while using the proxy configuration only for the authentication service.

 

 

Requesting a Hyperscaler Load Balancer for Outbound Traffic

The TCPS protocol is required for the AMQP connectivity configured in the Event Channel. So, a solution is to establish this connection directly using a Hyperscaler Load Balancer. 

For that open a Service Request using the template Create Hyperscaler LB for OUTBOUND traffic to an EXTERNAL source(Azure/AWS/GCP).

 

Select the SAP S/4HANA system that needs to be connected with the AEM broker and insert the requested parameters:

Customer ID - the SAP S/4HANA system that needs to be connected.

Select Hyperscaler - the hyperscaler where the selected SAP S/4HANA system is hosted.

Primary or DR Site - if the service is to be performed on the Primary or the DR site.

Source IP Addresses - the IP address of the selected SAP S/4HANA system.

Destination IP Addresses - the Solace broker instance IP address or the host specified with protocol prefix: tcps://<hostname>

Destination Ports - 5671 (port used for AMQP connectivity)

 

Confirm and schedule service.

 

Updating the OAuth profile in SAP S/4HANA

You have created a AEM channel using a service key and the activation fails with the following error:

You have checked the OAuth configuration using the report OA2C_GENERIC_ACCESS, as described in KBA 3444844, and you are receiving the following error:

>> lcl_oa2c_app->EXECUTE_ATR
granttype Client Credentials configured.

HTTP failure, processing failed, invalid state, invalid timeout or others
oa2c_error
Client configuration error or network problems. See kernel traces.

 

Run transaction OA2C_CONFIG:

1. Select the Configuration Name created by your channel.

2. Under tab Administration - Access Settings - Add the values for proxy host and proxy port:

Save the OAuth2 Profile.

 

Run transaction SM59:

1. Select the type G destination created by your channel for the BTP AEM Validation Service.

2. Under Technical Settings - HTTP Proxy Options - Add the values for Proxy Host and Proxy Service:

Save and close.

 

3. Select the type G destination that you created for the Solace broker.

4. Under Technical Settings - Target System Settings - Maintain the same Host and Port used in Destination IP Addresses and Destination Ports requested in the SR Template Create Hyperscaler LB for OUTBOUND traffic to an EXTERNAL source(Azure/AWS/GCP).

 

5. Under Technical Settings - Target System Settings - HTTP Proxy Options - Remove the values for Proxy Host and Service:

Save and close.

 

Go to transaction  SE38 and execute the report OA2C_GENERIC_ACCESS again. Then confirm that you receive the following message:

 

 

Activating the AEM Channel in SAP S/4HANA

Run transaction IWXBE/CONFIG and activate your channel. Once the channel has been activated, perform a connectivity test.

If your channel activation fails, go to transaction SMICM and check the error in the Trace File.

entry.title: ABAP AI - Chapter 3: We go agentic!
entry.content:
Summary

  • SAP Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities will be supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in all releases as of 2021 with planned start in Q2/2026
  • SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities will be priced on a consumption basis via AI Units
  • ABAP Development Tools for VS Code are planned to be GA in Q2/2026
  • An ABAP MCP Server will be shipped in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse and ABAP Development Tools for VS Code as of Q2/2026 enabling agentic ABAP development experience
  • An ABAP Custom Code Transformation agent focusing on the migration from SAP ECC towards SAP S/4HANA is planned for Q2/2026

Intro

Since the start of ABAP AI in early 2025 (“Joule speaks ABAP!”), the update in September 2025 (“ABAP AI – Chapter 2”), now it is time to deliver on the promises made at SAP TechEd 2025 (“Introducing the next era of ABAP development”) – time for chapter 3!

We are entering the agentic era of ABAP development: let's get started.

SAP Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities in lower releases

As of June 2026, Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities are offered through a running on SAP BTP. This hub service combines authentication, license check, access to SAP BTP AI Core and use case specific business logic and can be plugged directly to the developer’s IDE. This way, we can offer ABAP AI capabilities mostly independent of the ABAP backend release. This enables us to serve SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition on releases 2021 and higher with Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities.

  • Planned availability in Q2/26
  • Supported releases: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2021 and higher
  • Costs: See below
  • Configuration guide and further information will follow and will be linked here

New commercial approach for ABAP AI

The free promotion for J4D ABAP AI capabilities comes to an end in September 2026. Accordingly, it is now time to launch the commercial model: Based on AI units, ABAP AI capabilities are now priced purely based on consumption. This approach combines transparency regarding the real costs based on the usage and is well-suited for dynamic and individual agentic AI use cases.

  • Planned availability in Q2/26
  • Materials: 8019164 (customers) and 8019670 (partners)
  • Information on how to purchase and activate will follow soon and will be linked here

ABAP Development Tools for VS Code

14 years after the launch of ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse, time has come to support a new IDE. As Q2 2026, ABAP Development Tools will be available for VS Code. Building on the ABAP Language Server (see “Behind the Design…”), coupled with an ABAP MCP Server, this is the enabler for a truly agentic ABAP development experience, boosting development efficiency to the next level. It sets the foundation to leverage modern coding agents like GitHub Copilot directly for ABAP development. While we start with a small set of development objects and IDE tools focusing on SAP Fiori App development based on the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP), we will very soon enhance the scope of object types to serve high priority development scenarios.

The VS Code extension is

  • planned to be available as of Q2/26
  • Supported releases: release 7.31 and higher
  • Works in the Cloud and OnPrem
  • Costs: free of charge
  • Precise info on how to obtain and get started will follow soon and will be linked here

MCP Support for ABAP development

The Language Server provides the required abstraction to gain independence of the IDE sitting on top. A major component of this delivery is the MCP server for ABAP development. It provides powerful tools which help coding agents to perform complex ABAP coding tasks.
